mapping.a_element: nonlinear Schrodinger equation governing the optical wave in the nonlinear medium
mapping.b_element: wave propagation equation in the nonlinear Kerr medium

mapping.a_element: partially incoherent plane wave with random phase fluctuations
mapping.b_element: partially coherent wave characterized by its incoherent field spectrum

mapping.a_element: evolution equation for the Wigner transform (kinetic/transport equation for the field's spectral density)
mapping.b_element: dispersion relation for modulational instability as a functional of the incoherence spectrum

mapping.a_element: Landau-like damping from the spread of random phases
mapping.b_element: spectral-width dependence that suppresses (or enhances) the modulational-instability growth rate

mapping.a_element: stabilization / modification of the modulational instability of the incoherent field
mapping.b_element: modification of the range and existence of modulationally unstable wave numbers

brief_justification: Both perform a linear stability (modulational-instability) analysis of a partially incoherent field governed by the nonlinear Schrodinger/Kerr equation, in which the growth rate follows from an integral dispersion relation set by the field's incoherence spectrum; paper A's Wigner-transport-derived Landau-like damping is exactly the spectrum-dependent mechanism whose non-monotonic effect on the unstable band paper B explores, so the same kinetic MI machinery drives both.
